title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Urban gardening has seen a (0) REMARKABLE (REMARK) rise in popularity over the last few years. Transforming (21) VACANT (VACANCY) lots into green spaces provides (18) NUMEROUS (NUMBER) benefits for city dwellers. These gardens encourage the (19) PRODUCTION (PRODUCE) of fresh, local food and reduce the (20) RELIANCE (RELY) on long-distance transport. Furthermore, they offer (21) EDUCATIONAL (EDUCATION) opportunities for children to learn about nature. The (22) INVOLVEMENT (INVOLVE) of the local community is (23) ESSENTIAL (ESSENCE) for the success of these projects. (24) UNFORTUNATELY (FORTUNE), finding suitable space in crowded cities can be difficult. However, with creative (25) SOLUTIONS (SOLVE) like rooftop gardens, urban areas can become greener and more sustainable. These initiatives not only improve air quality but also foster a sense of pride and cooperation among neighbors, turning concrete jungles into vibrant ecosystems that support both human well-being and local wildlife, ensuring a healthier future for all residents and the planet.

example.text: Urban gardening has seen a (0) REMARKABLE (REMARK) rise...
example.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'rise'; REMARK → REMARKABLE.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'lots'; VACANCY → VACANT.
Adjective required to modify 'benefits'; NUMBER → NUMEROUS.
Adverb with a negative prefix required; FORTUNE → UNFORTUNATELY.
